mnemonics: L.I.D. R.I.P. for traversals: Level-order, In-order, Depth-first (Pre/Post). Remember: 'LID' keeps things contained, 'RIP' for the rest.
keyPoints: Traversal Order Matters: In-order (LNR) gives sorted values in BSTs, Pre-order (NLR) is for copying tree structure, Post-order (LRN) is for deletion.
Insertion & Search: Always start at the root and traverse down, comparing values to decide left or right (in a BST).
Deletion has Three Cases: Leaf node (just remove), node with one child (bypass it), node with two children (replace with inorder successor/predecessor).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the 'node with two children' case properly during deletion, which can break the tree's structure.
Assuming a binary tree is a Binary Search Tree (BST); operations like search and insertion rely on the BST ordering property (left < root < right).
